evals.name: real-pr-6304-epki
evals.prompt: Do a thorough, adversarial review of this PR: branch origin/feat/certificate targeting feat/macos-sys-ext (already fetched locally), focused on the certificate feature. Read the PR description but verify against the code.
files: git ref origin/feat/certificate (EPKI files: pkcs11/index.ts, alias.ts, CtkToken.mm, OVPNKeychainHelper.mm, CoreVpnClient.cpp, SystemTunnelAdapter.mm, migrations.js)

assertions.name: found_single_identity_rebind
assertions.text: Surfaces the resolveCtkKeyid single-identity fallback that silently rebinds a profile to an unverified cert (desktop/src/main/pkcs11/index.ts).

assertions.name: found_uaf
assertions.text: Surfaces the use-after-free: caller-stack out-param written by a late NSXPC reply/error block in the sync agent helpers (OVPNKeychainHelper.mm).

assertions.name: found_alias_dispatch
assertions.text: Surfaces the EPKI alias dispatch problem in CoreVpnClient.cpp: two independent if-ladders that must stay lock-step and/or the silent keychain fallthrough on an unrecognized alias.

assertions.name: flagged_stale_description
assertions.text: Notes that the PR description describes a superseded/reverted design and should be reviewed against the final code.

assertions.name: killed_frozen_state_fp
assertions.text: Does NOT assert as a real bug that migration 70 throws on frozen state (FP: migrations run pre-hydration).

assertions.name: killed_unique_ptr_fp
assertions.text: Does NOT assert as a real bug a provideCredentials unique_ptr data race introduced by this PR (FP: pre-existing, untouched).

assertions.name: severity_calibrated
assertions.text: The use-after-free is rated medium (not high — narrow trigger) and the userAgentConnection atomicity item, if present, is rated low/nit (mitigated by the retry path) — i.e. severity is calibrated, not inflated.

assertions.name: grounded_file_line
assertions.text: Findings reference concrete file:line or function names.

evals.name: synthetic-connection-pool
evals.prompt: Do a thorough review of this PR adding an async connection pool. Code at <inputs>/connectionPool.ts, PR description at <inputs>/PR_DESCRIPTION.md. Read the description but verify against the code.

assertions.name: found_cap_race
assertions.text: Identifies that acquire()'s `conns.length >= MAX` check followed by openConn()'s `await handshake` before `conns.push` lets two concurrent acquire() calls both pass the check and exceed MAX (check-then-await race breaking the cap invariant).

assertions.name: found_lastused_stale
assertions.text: Identifies that release() does not update lastUsed, so closeIdle() measures idleness from acquire time — a long-in-use connection released now is immediately eligible for eviction (wrong idle accounting).

assertions.name: killed_iterate_filter_fp
assertions.text: Does NOT report closeIdle()'s `conns = conns.filter(...)` as a concurrent-modification-during-iteration bug — the for...of loop completes before the reassignment, so it is safe. (false-positive trap)

assertions.name: severity_calibrated
assertions.text: Severity is calibrated: the cap race / idle-accounting bugs are medium-ish (real but bounded), nothing is over-rated critical, and the trap is not present as a finding.

assertions.name: grounded_file_line
assertions.text: Findings reference concrete lines or functions in connectionPool.ts.
